Convert & Download
Supported inputs: PDF, Word (DOCX), PowerPoint (PPTX) and plain text. Any file can be converted to PDF, Word, PowerPoint, Markdown or plain text. One output format applies to every file. For cross-format conversions (such as PDF → Word or PowerPoint), the text content is rebuilt into the new format — the original layout and images aren't preserved.

Build the Perfect Prompt
Every Time
New to AI? You're in the right place. Tell us what you want, pick your AI assistant, and we'll write a clear, structured prompt for you using the proven PEFTC framework — Persona, Exemplar, Format, Task, Tone, Context.
01 Describe your goal

Tap any step to jump there. Each one builds on the last.
The skill that changes everything
## The 3 Levels of Prompting
What you type on the surface vs. what actually gets results. Most people stay at Level 1. This builder takes you all the way to Level 3 — where the best work happens.
1
Just type On the surface
Where most people stop
No setup — just type your question and hope for the best
Give it one sample: “write it like this”
Quick one-off jobs: fix, shorten, translate
Ask for a list, then pick the best one
2
Give the full picture Going deeper
Where real work happens
Give it a character — who it is, how it should sound
Be specific about the exact job & who reads it
Tell it how to reply — bullets, a table, paragraphs
Paste a finished example to match
Set hard rules — always do this, never do that
Wire in your tools & files
3
Where results live Going all in
Everything changes here
Pick the right model for the task
Tell it to think first, before it replies
Turn on Plan Mode — approve the plan, then it goes
Keep your context in one place (a reusable context file)
Turn repeat jobs into reusable Skills & Projects
Connect your tools so it reads & writes for you
Stack it all: role + context + examples + iteration
Most people live at Level 1 . Everything changes at Level 3 — and the steps below take you there.
🤖
Step 2 · Choose Your AI Assistant
Pick the AI tool you'll paste your prompt into. We'll open it for you in a new tab.
Why this matters: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini and the rest each have a preferred prompt style. Selecting your AI here lets us assemble the prompt in the structure that AI responds to best — Claude gets the "DNA" structure, the others get clean PEFTC.

## Step 1 · What do you want your AI to do?

Why this matters: a clear goal is the difference between a mediocre AI reply and a usable one. We use what you type here as the seed for all six PEFTC tiles below — they fill in live as you type , so you can see your prompt take shape in real time.

✓ Copied to clipboard
## How to use this — in 5 simple steps
Brand new to AI? Follow these and you'll write better prompts than 90% of users on day one.
01
Pick your AI tool.
Choose ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, Perplexity, or Meta AI — whichever you have access to.
02
Describe your goal in plain words.
Type what you want — like writing to a smart colleague. Be specific about the result you need.
03
Hit Generate prompt.
The advisor fills in all six tiles — Persona, Exemplar, Format, Task, Tone, Context — for you.
04
Tweak any tile.
Edit any tile to match your exact needs. The final prompt updates live as you type.
05
Copy and paste into your AI.
Click Copy final prompt , then Open AI , and paste. Done — instant pro-level results.
## ✶ The Only Claude AI Cheat Sheet You'll Ever Need
A quick reference for getting the most out of Claude — models, the Context File, Memory, Skills, Connectors, Scheduled Tasks, prompting techniques, copy-paste prompts, and Claude vs ChatGPT. Tap any card to expand.

# https://okiru.biz/bizbrain/

**Page title:** Okiru BizBrain — All-In-One Business Tool for South Africa
**Meta description:** Okiru BizBrain is the all in one app for small business South Africa. Leads, invoicing, client messages, social media and your blog in one place from R999pm.

OKIRU
AI Tool Advisor
All Tools

Home › Okiru BizBrain
Okiru BizBrain · From R999pm
## Run your business from one place, not five apps.
Leads, invoicing, client messages, social media and your blog, sorted. BizBrain is a website and business intelligence tool in one, custom built around your company and brand.
Book a demo
WhatsApp us

Every BizBrain is custom built for your business. No off-the-shelf templates.

New lead from your website — Thandi M. requested a quote
## Five apps. Five bills. Nothing talks to each other.
If you run a hair business, a food business or any side hustle in South Africa, your admin probably looks like this:
Leads arrive on WhatsApp and get lost in the chat history
Invoices live in Zoho or a spreadsheet you update at midnight
Social media posts sit in Buffer, when you remember to schedule them
Your website was built once and never updated
Every one of these tools charges you separately, in dollars
You did not start your business to do admin across five apps. That is the problem BizBrain removes.
The fix
## One app that manages leads, invoicing and social media together
Okiru BizBrain replaces the stack. It is an AI business tool for small business South Africa that keeps everything about your business in one place, and gets smarter about your business the longer you use it.
📥
## Lead capture & CRM
Every enquiry lands in one list with a name, a number and a next step. No more lost WhatsApp leads.
🧾
## Invoicing
Send professional invoices and quotes in rand, track who has paid, and chase the ones who have not.
💬
## Client communication
Follow-ups, booking confirmations and thank-you messages, drafted for you in your tone.
📅
## Social media scheduling
Plan a month of posts in one sitting. BizBrain writes drafts around your products and specials.
📝
## Blog & content publishing
An AI powered website presence without a web designer. Publish updates and specials your customers can find on Google.
🧠
## Learns your business
BizBrain studies your sales, clients and busy periods, then suggests what to do next. That is AI business intelligence working for a small business, not just a big one.
Inside BizBrain
## This is what you get: your whole business on one screen
Your own admin portal with a live dashboard, analytics, bookings, contacts, email, blog and an AI content engine. Here is a taste of what it looks like.

Getting started
## How it works
1
## Register your business details
Tell BizBrain what you sell, who your customers are and how you like to work. Plain questions, no jargon.
2
## Connect your accounts
Link your bank and social media accounts so invoices, payments and posts flow through one place.
3
## Let it run and learn
BizBrain handles the admin and learns from your data, so its suggestions get better every month.
Who it is for
## Built for the business you run, not the tech you know
You do not need to understand tech. BizBrain is built for you: it asks plain questions about your business and does the technical work behind the scenes.
💅
## Beauty industry
Salons, nail techs, lash artists, skincare brands and mobile beauticians. Keep bookings, client follow-ups and your Instagram presence running while you work with clients.
🛍️
## Selling online
Products or services sold through Instagram, WhatsApp or your own site. Capture every enquiry, invoice in rand and keep content going out so sales do not depend on you posting at midnight.
🚀
## Side hustle or main hustle
Really, any business you want to run alongside a job or grow into your main income. BizBrain gives you the guidance and frees up your time, so the admin never becomes the reason you stop.
Case study
## Woman of Taste runs on BizBrain
Visit womanoftaste.co.za ↗
Woman of Taste is a South African events and experiences business that used to juggle booking requests on WhatsApp, payments in a spreadsheet and social media whenever there was a spare hour. It is exactly the kind of business BizBrain was built for: excellent at the product, drowning in the admin. The website you see above is BizBrain too: her public site and admin portal are one custom-built system.
Today the whole business runs from one BizBrain admin portal: members, events and ticket bookings, a contact list with newsletter sign-ups, email, blog and social media publishing, an AI content engine, project management and a live analytics dashboard that tracks revenue against monthly targets.
R15 600 collected from a single event, tracked end to end
24 tickets sold across 18 bookings
75% of booking requests converted to paid
1 place for bookings, payments, contacts & content
Figures from the Woman of Taste BizBrain analytics dashboard, event: The Devil Wears Prada II.

The maths
## BizBrain vs running it all separately
Most small businesses end up paying separately for QuickBooks, HubSpot, Mailchimp, Buffer, Canva, Wix and ChatGPT — and none of them talk to each other. BizBrain is the affordable alternative built for South Africa: one subscription that replaces the whole stack.
What you need Popular tool Typical cost
Invoicing & accounting QuickBooks ± R550pm
CRM & lead tracking HubSpot ± R350pm
Email marketing Mailchimp ± R250pm
Social media scheduling Buffer ± R250pm
Social media management Hootsuite ± R550pm
Design & content Canva Pro ± R220pm
Website & blog Wix ± R350pm
AI assistant subscription ChatGPT Plus ± R400pm
Running it separately ± R2,900pm+
Okiru BizBrain, all of it in one app from R999pm
Comparison pricing is approximate, based on entry-level paid plans converted to rand at time of writing. QuickBooks, HubSpot, Mailchimp, Buffer, Hootsuite, Canva, Wix and ChatGPT are trademarks of their respective owners; BizBrain is not affiliated with them.
Questions
## Frequently asked questions
Is BizBrain for total beginners?
Yes. BizBrain was built for people who run a business, not people who run software. If you can use WhatsApp, you can use BizBrain. Setup asks you plain questions about your business and does the technical work for you.
Do I need a registered business to use BizBrain?
No. Side hustles are welcome. Whether you braid hair from home, sell food on weekends or run a registered company, BizBrain works the same way. You can add your registration and VAT details later if you formalise.
Can I cancel anytime?
Yes. BizBrain is month to month. There is no contract and no cancellation fee. If you cancel, you can export your client list, invoices and content before you go.
Does BizBrain work on my phone?
Yes. BizBrain runs in your phone's browser and is built mobile first, because most South African small business owners run their business from their phone. There is nothing to install.
What happens to my data?
Your data stays yours. BizBrain stores your client and business information securely, uses it only to run your business tools, and complies with POPIA. You can export or delete your data at any time.
How is BizBrain different from QuickBooks, Zoho or Wix?
QuickBooks does your invoicing, Zoho gives you separate tools you must connect yourself, and Wix builds your website — but each one is a separate subscription that knows nothing about the others. BizBrain gives you one system that already works together: leads, invoices, client messages, social media and your blog share the same information about your business, so nothing falls through the cracks.
Is BizBrain a QuickBooks or Mailchimp alternative?
For most South African small businesses, yes. BizBrain covers the essentials those tools are used for — quotes and invoices in rand, client contact lists and email updates — plus lead capture, social media and a website, all in one app from R999pm. If you need advanced accounting like payroll or SARS e-filing, your accountant can still export your BizBrain invoice data.
Is there a free trial?
No. We offer a demo instead, because every BizBrain system is custom built based on your company and brand. It is a website and business intelligence tool in one, so we walk you through exactly what yours would look like before you commit.
What do I need to get started?
Book a demo and bring your business details: what you sell, who your customers are and how you want to be paid. We then build your BizBrain around your company and brand, and it starts learning your business and doing the admin for you.
## Spend your time on the business, not the admin

# https://okiru.biz/blog/ai-agents-are-here-why-skills-development-must-move-from-training-to-workflow-readiness/

**Page title:** AI Agents Are Here: Why Skills Development Must Move From Training to Workflow Readiness | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** AI is no longer a productivity tool sitting beside employees — it's becoming part of the workflow itself. McKinsey found almost every company is investing in AI, yet only 1 percent feel mature. The gap isn't interest. It's readiness.

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
AI Agents Are Here: Why Skills Development Must Move From Training to Workflow Readiness
Future of Work
## AI Agents Are Here: Why Skills Development Must Move From Training to Workflow Readiness
08 June 2026 · 5 min read · By Okiru Consulting
AI is no longer just a productivity tool sitting beside employees. The big shift is that AI is becoming part of the workflow itself. In 2025, McKinsey found that almost all companies were investing in AI, but only 1 percent believed they had reached maturity. The gap is no longer interest. The gap is readiness.
## The New AI Reality
Generative AI has moved from simple content creation into agents, assistants, automation, decision support, customer service, coding, analytics, and knowledge management.
That changes the skills conversation.
Employees do not only need to know how to "use AI." They need to know how to work with AI responsibly, evaluate its outputs, redesign processes around it, and understand where human judgement still matters.
The World Economic Forum reports that AI, big data, cybersecurity, networks, and technological literacy are among the fastest growing skill priorities for employers.
## Why AI Adoption Is Not Enough
Many organizations have already introduced AI tools. But adoption does not automatically create value.
The real challenge is whether people know how to apply AI in the flow of work.
Without the right skills, AI can create:
Faster mistakes
Poor quality outputs
Unclear accountability
Data and compliance risks
Employees who use AI without understanding its limits
McKinsey's 2025 workplace AI report argues that the biggest barrier to scaling AI is not employee resistance, but leadership and organizational readiness.
## The Rise of Workflow Skills
The most valuable AI skills in 2026 will not be limited to prompt writing.
High impact AI skills include:
AI assisted problem solving
Critical evaluation of AI outputs
Data literacy
Process redesign
Responsible AI usage
Cybersecurity awareness
Human judgement and decision making
Change readiness
This is where many learning strategies need to evolve. Training employees on isolated tools is useful, but it is not enough. Organizations need to map AI skills directly to roles, workflows, risks, and business outcomes.
## Why This Matters for Business Leaders
The IMF notes that AI is reshaping work and that workers need support to adapt, acquire new skills, and move into emerging opportunities.
That means the organizations that win with AI will not simply be the ones buying the best tools. They will be the ones building the strongest workforce capability around those tools.
AI success depends on people who can ask better questions, interpret better answers, make better decisions, and apply technology with purpose.
## What Organizations Should Do Next
Instead of launching broad AI training for everyone, organizations should focus on targeted capability building.
A stronger approach would be to:
Identify which roles are most affected by AI
Define the AI skills each role actually needs
Assess current readiness
Build practical learning pathways
Measure improvement through workplace outcomes
Review skills continuously as AI tools evolve
This turns AI learning from a once off training exercise into a living skills strategy.
## The Okiru Perspective
For Okiru, the message is clear: AI transformation is really a skills transformation.
Organizations do not need more generic training. They need clearer visibility into workforce capability, smarter skills mapping, and development programs connected to measurable impact.
The future of AI at work will belong to organizations that can answer one simple question:
Do our people have the right skills to turn AI investment into real business value?
AI Skills Workforce Readiness AI Agents Future of Work Skills Development Responsible AI Change Management Workflow Automation

# https://okiru.biz/blog/ai-and-cybersecurity-the-new-digital-arms-race/

**Page title:** AI and Cybersecurity: The New Digital Arms Race | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** The same AI defending your business is being weaponised against it. Attackers now use generative AI to clone voices, write malware, and run phishing at scale — while defenders use AI to detect threats in milliseconds. Welcome to cybersecurity's machine-speed era.

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
AI and Cybersecurity: The New Digital Arms Race
Cybersecurity
## AI and Cybersecurity: The New Digital Arms Race
25 May 2026 · 7 min read · By Okiru Consulting
Artificial Intelligence is transforming cybersecurity at an unprecedented pace. But there is a growing problem.
The same AI technologies helping organisations defend against cyber threats are also being used by attackers to create more advanced, scalable, and intelligent attacks.
This has created a new digital arms race.
On one side, businesses and governments are deploying AI-powered defense systems capable of detecting threats in real time. On the other, cybercriminals are using AI to automate phishing, generate malware, bypass security controls, and launch increasingly sophisticated attacks.
The result is a cybersecurity landscape that is evolving faster than ever before.
## AI is Changing Cybercrime
Cyberattacks are no longer limited to highly skilled hackers working manually. AI is lowering the barrier to entry and accelerating the speed of attacks.
Threat actors are now using generative AI tools to:
Create convincing phishing emails in seconds
Clone voices for fraud and impersonation
Generate malicious code
Automate reconnaissance and vulnerability scanning
Launch social engineering attacks at scale
According to Microsoft's Cyber Signals report, AI-assisted fraud and cybercrime are increasing significantly, with attackers using AI to improve both the quality and speed of attacks.
Deepfake scams have also become a growing concern. In several reported cases, employees were tricked into transferring money after receiving AI-generated voice or video calls impersonating executives.
This is changing the nature of cyber risk entirely.
## AI is Also Strengthening Cyber Defense
While attackers are becoming smarter, cybersecurity teams are also using AI to strengthen defenses.
Modern AI-driven security systems can:
Detect suspicious behaviour in real time
Identify anomalies across massive datasets
Automate incident response
Predict emerging threats
Reduce response times during attacks
Google Cloud recently reported that AI-enhanced threat detection is becoming essential as organisations face increasingly complex cyber environments. AI allows security teams to process enormous amounts of data far faster than human analysts alone ever could.
In practice, this means organisations can respond to threats earlier and more effectively.
## The Rise of Autonomous Cybersecurity
One of the biggest trends emerging now is autonomous cybersecurity .
Instead of waiting for human intervention, AI systems are beginning to independently:
Monitor networks
Isolate suspicious activity
Block attacks automatically
Recommend remediation steps
Learn from evolving threat patterns
This shift is especially important because cyberattacks are happening at machine speed.
According to IBM's Cost of a Data Breach Report , organisations with advanced AI security systems reduce breach detection and containment times significantly compared to those without AI capabilities. The faster an organisation responds, the lower the operational and financial damage.
## The Risks of AI-Driven Security
However, AI cybersecurity systems are not perfect. AI models themselves can become targets.
Security experts are increasingly warning about threats such as:
Prompt injection attacks
AI model manipulation
Data poisoning
Adversarial attacks
AI hallucinations in security decisions
A recent report from the World Economic Forum highlighted that while AI improves cybersecurity capabilities, it also expands the attack surface for organisations worldwide. This means businesses cannot rely on automation alone.
Human oversight, governance, and cybersecurity expertise remain critical.
## What This Means for Africa
Africa's rapid digital transformation makes this conversation especially important.
As businesses, banks, fintechs, healthcare providers, and governments expand digital services, the continent is becoming increasingly exposed to cyber threats. At the same time, many organisations still face challenges around:
Cybersecurity skills shortages
Legacy infrastructure
Limited cybersecurity investment
Low digital awareness
AI presents a major opportunity to strengthen cyber resilience across African industries. Automated security systems can help organisations scale protection even when cybersecurity resources are limited.
However, responsible implementation will be essential. The future of cybersecurity in Africa will depend not only on adopting AI, but on building secure digital ecosystems, investing in talent, and creating strong governance frameworks.
## Final Thoughts
Artificial Intelligence is reshaping cybersecurity from both sides of the battlefield.
Attackers are becoming faster, more automated, and more sophisticated. Defenders are using AI to detect threats earlier, automate responses, and improve resilience. This escalation is only beginning.
In the years ahead, cybersecurity may become less about humans versus hackers and more about AI systems defending against AI-driven attacks .
For organisations, the challenge is clear: the future belongs to businesses that can innovate quickly while building security into every layer of their digital transformation journey.
Because in the age of AI, cybersecurity is no longer just an IT issue. It is a business survival issue.
Okiru Consulting helps South African organisations build AI-ready cybersecurity strategies — covering threat modelling, governance, POPIA alignment, and human-in-the-loop defence design. Get in touch via okiru.co.za .

# https://okiru.biz/blog/ai-in-2026-the-biggest-changes-happening-right-now-and-what-they-mean-for-business/

**Page title:** AI in 2026: The Biggest Changes Happening Right Now and What They Mean for Business | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** A year ago the conversation was chatbots and content generation. Today it's AI agents, autonomous workflows, multimodal intelligence, and AI moving into the physical world. Here are the most significant trends shaping 2026 — and what they mean for business.

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
AI in 2026: The Biggest Changes Happening Right Now and What They Mean for Business
AI Trends
## AI in 2026: The Biggest Changes Happening Right Now and What They Mean for Business
02 June 2026 · 8 min read · By Okiru Consulting
Artificial Intelligence is evolving faster than ever. Just a year ago, most discussions centred around chatbots and content generation. Today, the conversation has shifted toward AI agents, autonomous workflows, multimodal intelligence, and entirely new ways of working.
As organisations continue to explore the opportunities presented by AI, understanding the latest developments is becoming essential for business leaders, technology teams, and professionals across every industry. Here are some of the most significant AI trends shaping 2026 and what they mean for the future.
## AI Agents Are Moving Beyond Chat
One of the biggest shifts in 2026 is the rise of AI agents .
Unlike traditional AI assistants that respond to prompts, AI agents can plan, reason, use tools, gather information, and complete multi-step tasks with limited human intervention. Major technology companies are now investing heavily in agent-based ecosystems designed to automate complex workflows and business processes. This shift is changing the role of AI from simply answering questions to actively helping people complete work.
Examples include:
Research and analysis
Customer service automation
Project coordination
Software development support
Data processing and reporting
Supply chain monitoring
The future workplace is increasingly likely to include teams made up of both people and AI agents working together.
## The Era of Multimodal AI Has Arrived
Modern AI systems are becoming far more capable of understanding multiple forms of information simultaneously.
Rather than working only with text, today's AI models can process:
Documents
Images
Audio
Video
Data visualisations
Code
This capability, known as multimodal AI , enables richer interactions and more accurate insights. Businesses can now analyse reports, presentations, spreadsheets, images, and conversations within a single workflow. As these capabilities improve, AI will become increasingly integrated into everyday business operations.
## AI Models Are Becoming More Specialised
Another major trend is the move away from one-size-fits-all AI models.
Technology companies are increasingly developing specialised models designed for specific tasks such as coding, cybersecurity, scientific research, image generation, transcription, and enterprise workflows. This allows organisations to choose AI solutions that are better suited to their particular needs rather than relying on a single general-purpose system.
The result is greater efficiency, improved performance, and more targeted business outcomes.
## The Competition Between AI Leaders Is Intensifying
The global AI race is accelerating.
Major players including OpenAI, Microsoft, Google, Anthropic, Meta, Nvidia, and a growing number of open-source communities are investing billions into AI research, infrastructure, and talent acquisition.
Recent developments include:
Microsoft's launch of new in-house reasoning models and AI platforms.
Nvidia's expansion into world models and agent-focused computing platforms.
Meta's significant investment in next-generation AI research and infrastructure.
Rapid advances in open-source AI models that are becoming increasingly competitive with commercial offerings.
This competition is driving faster innovation and making advanced AI capabilities more accessible than ever before.
## Governance and Responsible AI Are Becoming Critical
As AI systems become more autonomous, governance is becoming a top priority. Businesses are increasingly focused on questions such as:
How do we ensure AI outputs are reliable?
How do we protect sensitive information?
How do we monitor autonomous AI agents?
How do we comply with emerging regulations?
Researchers and organisations are developing new approaches to AI governance that focus on monitoring actions, enforcing policies, and ensuring accountability throughout AI-driven workflows.
The future of AI will depend not only on capability but also on trust.
## AI Is Expanding Into the Physical World
For years, AI primarily existed in software applications. That is beginning to change.
Advances in robotics, autonomous systems, and world models are enabling AI to interact more effectively with physical environments. New AI systems can better understand movement, objects, environments, and real-world decision-making.
Industries such as manufacturing, logistics, healthcare, transportation, and infrastructure management are expected to benefit significantly from these developments over the coming years.
## The Most Valuable Skill Is Adaptability
Despite all the technological advancements, one thing remains clear: people remain central to the future of work.
AI can analyse data, automate processes, and generate insights. Humans provide judgement, creativity, ethics, leadership, and context. As AI becomes more capable, the demand for skills such as critical thinking, communication, problem solving, and digital literacy will continue to grow.
The organisations and individuals who succeed will be those who embrace continuous learning and understand how to effectively collaborate with AI.
## Looking Ahead
AI is no longer a future trend. It is a present reality that is transforming how businesses operate and how people work.
The biggest story of 2026 is not simply that AI is becoming more powerful. It is that AI is becoming more practical, more autonomous, and more deeply integrated into everyday workflows. For organisations, the opportunity lies in moving beyond experimentation and focusing on responsible implementation, workforce readiness, and long-term value creation.
The future will belong to those who can successfully combine human expertise with intelligent technology. The age of Human + AI collaboration has officially begun.
Okiru Consulting helps South African organisations move from AI experimentation to responsible implementation — covering strategy, agent workflows, governance, and workforce readiness. Get in touch via okiru.co.za .

# https://okiru.biz/blog/ai-in-2026-the-biggest-shifts-every-business-should-be-watching/

**Page title:** AI in 2026: The Biggest Shifts Every Business Should Be Watching | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** AI is no longer evolving one model at a time — it's evolving how businesses operate. Here are the five shifts every organisation should be watching in 2026.

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
AI in 2026: The Biggest Shifts Every Business Should Be Watching
AI Trends
## AI in 2026: The Biggest Shifts Every Business Should Be Watching
29 June 2026 · 6 min read · By Okiru Consulting
AI is no longer evolving one model at a time. It is evolving the way businesses operate.
Over the past few weeks alone, the AI industry has experienced significant developments that point to a clear trend: organisations are moving away from simply using AI as a chatbot and towards integrating AI as an operational partner.
For South African businesses, this is more than another technology update. It is an opportunity to rethink productivity, decision making, compliance, and competitive advantage.
## 1. AI Is Becoming More Agentic
One of the biggest developments in 2026 is the rise of agentic AI.
Unlike traditional AI assistants that wait for instructions, agentic AI systems can complete multi-step tasks, coordinate workflows, remember context, and work towards goals with limited supervision.
Research published in June 2026 shows rapid adoption of agent-based AI within organisations, with users increasingly assigning complex tasks that would traditionally take hours or even days to complete.
This means AI is moving beyond:
Writing emails
Summarising documents
Answering questions
Instead, organisations are beginning to use AI to:
Prepare reports
Manage projects
Analyse business data
Coordinate research
Support operational decision making
This represents a major shift from AI as a tool to AI as a digital teammate.
## 2. Competition Between AI Companies Is Accelerating Innovation
The competition between OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and emerging global AI providers has intensified significantly.
Recent weeks have seen:
New frontier AI model releases
Continued improvements in reasoning capabilities
Faster coding assistants
Better multimodal understanding
Lower operating costs for enterprise AI deployments
At the same time, talent movement between major AI companies highlights how aggressively organisations are investing in next-generation AI capabilities.
For businesses, increased competition is good news. It means:
Better AI performance
More choice
Lower pricing over time
Faster innovation cycles
Organisations are no longer locked into a single AI provider.
## 3. AI Governance Is Becoming a Boardroom Issue
Recent regulatory developments have demonstrated that governments are taking advanced AI far more seriously than ever before.
In the United States, advanced frontier AI releases have recently undergone additional government cybersecurity review before wider deployment.
Whether similar regulatory approaches emerge elsewhere or not, one message is becoming clear: businesses cannot deploy AI without governance.
Organisations need clear policies covering:
Data privacy
Human oversight
Information security
AI ethics
Risk management
Compliance monitoring
For South African businesses, this aligns directly with obligations under POPIA, Employment Equity, B-BBEE verification, and sector-specific regulatory requirements.
## 4. AI Is Becoming a Global Competitive Race
AI leadership is no longer concentrated in one region.
Recent reports show Chinese AI companies rapidly closing performance gaps with leading Western models while releasing increasingly capable open models.
For businesses this means:
More AI platforms to evaluate
Greater pricing competition
Faster innovation worldwide
Increased importance of selecting trusted enterprise AI providers
Choosing an AI platform will increasingly become a strategic business decision rather than simply selecting whichever chatbot is most popular.
## 5. Skills Are Becoming the Real Competitive Advantage
The biggest difference between companies succeeding with AI and those struggling is no longer access to technology. It is people.
Organisations investing in AI literacy, prompt engineering, workflow design, and responsible AI use consistently achieve better results than businesses relying solely on technology.
The companies creating competitive advantage are training employees to:
Work alongside AI
Validate AI outputs
Design AI-assisted workflows
Improve decision making
Automate repetitive processes safely
AI expertise is quickly becoming a core business capability rather than an IT speciality.
## What This Means for South African Businesses
Most South African organisations have already experimented with AI. The next challenge is moving from experimentation to structured implementation.
Businesses should now focus on:
Identifying high-value AI use cases
Establishing AI governance frameworks
Training employees to use AI effectively
Integrating AI into existing business processes
Ensuring compliance with South African legislation and industry standards
Organisations that treat AI as a long-term operational capability rather than a short-term productivity tool will be best positioned to compete over the coming years.
## How Okiru Can Help
At Okiru, we help organisations move beyond simply using AI. We help businesses implement AI responsibly through:
AI Strategy and Implementation
Claude and ChatGPT Training
Microsoft Copilot Enablement
AI Governance and Policy Development
POPIA-Aware AI Adoption
AI Workflow Design and Business Process Improvement
Executive AI Advisory
Whether your goal is improving productivity, strengthening compliance, or building AI into your transformation strategy, our team can help you implement AI with confidence.
Ready to transform the way your business works? Visit okiru.co.za or contact contact@okiru.co.za to learn more about our AI consulting and enterprise AI training services.

# https://okiru.biz/blog/ai-won-t-replace-you-but-your-competitor-s-ai-might/

**Page title:** AI Won't Replace You — But Your Competitor's AI Might | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** The jobs-versus-AI debate is a distraction. The real question is whether your competitors are moving faster than you, and whether the gap is already too wide to close.

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
AI Won't Replace You — But Your Competitor's AI Might
Strategy
## AI Won't Replace You — But Your Competitor's AI Might
7 April 2026 · 6 min read · By Okiru Consulting
The public conversation about AI almost always frames it as a human-versus-machine story. Will AI take my job? Will it replace writers, designers, lawyers, accountants? This framing makes for compelling headlines. It also completely misses the real business threat.
The danger isn't AI. The danger is your competitor using AI while you don't .
## The efficiency gap is real and growing
A marketing agency that uses AI to assist with brief interpretation, copy drafts, and performance analysis can produce comparable output to a larger team — faster and at lower cost. A law firm using AI to accelerate contract review and compliance checking can handle more clients without adding headcount. A training company using AI to personalise content delivery can serve more learners with better outcomes.
These aren't hypothetical futures. These are businesses operating right now, in your market, competing for the same clients. The efficiency gap they're building isn't just about cost — it's about capacity, speed, and the ability to take on work that would previously have required more resources .
The question is not whether AI will replace humans in your industry. The question is whether a human using AI will replace a human not using AI. In most industries, that answer is already yes.
## The compounding problem
What makes this particularly challenging is that AI adoption compounds. A team that has been using AI tools for 12 months hasn't just saved time — they've built institutional knowledge about which tools work for which tasks, developed judgment about when to trust AI outputs and when to verify, and created workflows that are significantly faster than their non-AI equivalents.
If you start today, you're already behind businesses that started a year ago. If you start in a year, the gap will be wider still.
## What to do about it
The answer isn't to panic and implement everything at once. It's to start deliberately and strategically. Pick one high-value, repeatable process in your business. Find the best AI tool for that specific use case. Implement it properly, measure the output, and use the learnings to expand.
Build momentum rather than waiting for certainty. The companies that will thrive in an AI-enabled market aren't necessarily the ones with the biggest budgets or the most ambitious transformation plans. They're the ones that started — imperfectly, incrementally, but consistently.
Your competitor isn't waiting. Neither should you.

# https://okiru.biz/blog/anthropic-s-latest-policy-changes-signal-a-new-era-of-ai-governance/

**Page title:** Anthropic's Latest Policy Changes Signal a New Era of AI Governance | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** Anthropic's recent policy moves — a stronger Responsible Scaling Policy, calls for government oversight, and greater transparency — offer a glimpse into where frontier AI governance is heading, and why it matters for every business.

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
Anthropic's Latest Policy Changes Signal a New Era of AI Governance
AI Governance
## Anthropic's Latest Policy Changes Signal a New Era of AI Governance
17 June 2026 · 6 min read · By Okiru Consulting
Artificial intelligence is moving from experimentation to critical infrastructure. Over the past few weeks, Anthropic has announced several significant policy changes and public positions that indicate where frontier AI governance may be heading.
While these developments are centred around one AI company, their implications extend far beyond Anthropic. They offer a glimpse into how regulators, enterprises, and AI providers may approach safety, transparency, accountability, and risk management in the years ahead.
## What Changed?
Anthropic's recent policy updates can be grouped into four major themes:
## 1. Stronger Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP)
Anthropic updated its Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP), the framework it uses to manage risks associated with increasingly capable AI systems. The latest revisions refine how the company evaluates biological and cybersecurity risks, introduce clearer procedures for responding to emerging threats, and expand reporting requirements around model safety.
The message is clear: as AI capabilities increase, governance mechanisms must evolve alongside them.
## 2. A Shift Toward Government Oversight
In a notable change of tone, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has moved beyond advocating for transparency requirements alone and is now calling for governments to have legal authority to block the deployment of dangerous frontier AI systems. The company has also proposed independent testing requirements, risk reporting obligations, and meaningful penalties for non-compliance.
This represents one of the strongest endorsements of formal AI regulation from a leading AI developer.
## 3. Increased Transparency Around Safety Controls
Anthropic recently faced criticism after implementing hidden safeguards that silently limited certain advanced AI development activities. Following feedback from researchers and developers, the company reversed course and committed to making such restrictions visible to users. Anthropic publicly acknowledged that it had "made the wrong tradeoff" between safety and transparency.
This highlights a growing industry expectation that AI providers must not only implement safeguards but also clearly communicate them.
## 4. Expanded Debate Around Security and Access
Recent government actions involving Anthropic's most advanced models have intensified discussions around national security, AI export controls, and access restrictions. Authorities raised concerns about potential cybersecurity risks and model misuse, leading to temporary restrictions on some advanced systems.
Whether these actions prove temporary or become standard practice, they demonstrate that governments increasingly view frontier AI as a strategic technology rather than simply a commercial product.
## Why This Matters for Businesses
Many organisations still view AI governance as a future concern. Anthropic's latest moves suggest that future may be arriving much faster than expected. Three implications stand out:
## Governance Will Become a Competitive Requirement
AI governance is no longer solely a compliance exercise. Customers, regulators, investors, and employees increasingly expect organisations to demonstrate responsible AI practices. Businesses that establish clear governance frameworks today will be better positioned as regulations mature.
## Transparency Is Becoming Essential
The backlash against hidden safeguards demonstrates a broader principle: users want visibility into how AI systems make decisions, apply restrictions, and manage risk. Organisations deploying AI internally should adopt the same philosophy. Transparent policies build trust and reduce operational risk.
## AI Risk Management Must Mature
Anthropic's policy updates focus heavily on cybersecurity, biological risks, model misuse, and system control. While most enterprises are not building frontier models, many are integrating AI into critical business processes. This means organisations need structured approaches for:
AI risk assessment
Data governance
Human oversight
Security monitoring
Compliance reporting
## What This Means for ESG and Responsible Business
For sustainability, governance, and risk leaders, these developments reinforce an important reality: AI governance is rapidly becoming a core component of corporate governance. The same principles that guide ESG programmes apply directly to AI adoption:
Accountability
Transparency
Risk management
Stakeholder trust
Long-term resilience
As regulators and industry leaders continue to shape AI policy, organisations that proactively govern AI will be better equipped to capture its benefits while managing emerging risks.
The conversation is moving beyond whether AI should be governed and toward how governance should be implemented.
## Looking Ahead
Anthropic's latest policy changes are not simply internal governance updates. They reflect a broader shift occurring across the AI ecosystem.
For businesses, the lesson is straightforward: responsible AI is no longer optional. It is becoming a fundamental requirement for sustainable digital transformation. The organisations that act early will likely find themselves better prepared for the next phase of AI adoption, regulation, and innovation.
Anthropic AI Governance Responsible Scaling Policy AI Regulation AI Transparency AI Risk Management ESG Frontier AI Dario Amodei

# https://okiru.biz/blog/anthropic-updates-its-privacy-policy-as-claude-s-capabilities-expand/

**Page title:** Anthropic Updates Its Privacy Policy as Claude's Capabilities Expand | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** As Claude takes on more complex, multi-step tasks and connects to third-party apps, Anthropic has refreshed its consumer privacy policy. Here's what changed, what stayed the same, and why it matters for anyone adopting AI responsibly.

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
Anthropic Updates Its Privacy Policy as Claude's Capabilities Expand
AI Policy
## Anthropic Updates Its Privacy Policy as Claude's Capabilities Expand
13 June 2026 · 4 min read · By Okiru Consulting
As artificial intelligence platforms continue to evolve, transparency around data handling remains critical. Anthropic has announced updates to its Privacy Policy to reflect Claude's growing capabilities — particularly its ability to perform more complex tasks and integrate with third-party applications.
The updated policy applies only to consumer accounts , including Claude Free, Pro, and Max plans. Business customers using Claude Team, Claude Enterprise, the Claude Platform, or services governed by Anthropic's Commercial Terms are not affected by these changes.
## Key Privacy Policy Updates
## 1. Multi-Step Tasks and Connected Applications
Claude is increasingly capable of carrying out multi-step workflows and interacting with external applications. Anthropic has expanded its Privacy Policy to explain how data may be shared between Claude and connected third-party services when users authorise these integrations.
This update provides greater clarity on how information moves between platforms when Claude performs actions on a user's behalf.
## 2. Age and Identity Verification
To support platform safety and security, Anthropic may request age or identity verification from certain users. The revised policy outlines what verification information may be collected and how it is processed.
This reflects a broader industry trend toward stronger identity assurance measures as AI services become more widely adopted.
## 3. Research and Study Participation
Users who choose to participate in surveys, interviews, research programs, or other studies conducted by Anthropic will now find more detailed explanations regarding the information collected during these activities.
The update improves transparency around voluntary participation in product research and development initiatives.
## 4. Expanded Details on Data Practices
Anthropic has also provided additional information about:
How it communicates with users
How service recommendations and product updates are delivered
Circumstances under which information may be shared with or received from third parties
The legal grounds used for processing personal information
These additions are intended to help users better understand how their data is handled across different aspects of the Claude ecosystem.
## What Hasn't Changed
While the Privacy Policy has evolved alongside Claude's expanding functionality, Anthropic emphasised that several core commitments remain unchanged:
User data is not sold
Claude remains free from advertising
Users retain control over whether their chats and coding sessions can be used to train and improve Anthropic's AI models
These commitments continue to differentiate Anthropic's approach in an increasingly competitive AI landscape.
## Why This Matters
As AI assistants become more capable of interacting with external systems and automating complex workflows, privacy policies must evolve to address new data flows and operational models.
For businesses, consumers, and technology leaders alike, understanding how AI providers collect, process, and share information is becoming an essential part of responsible AI adoption.
Anthropic's latest update reflects the growing maturity of AI platforms and highlights the importance of maintaining transparency as these technologies become more deeply integrated into everyday work and digital experiences.
Organisations using AI solutions should regularly review vendor privacy policies to ensure alignment with their governance, compliance, and data protection requirements.

# https://okiru.biz/blog/claude-cowork-your-ai-teammate-for-the-modern-workplace/

**Page title:** Claude Cowork: Your AI Teammate for the Modern Workplace | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** How Anthropic is transforming AI from a chatbot into a collaborative coworker — one that works alongside teams on real files, projects, and workflows.

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
Claude Cowork: Your AI Teammate for the Modern Workplace
AI at Work
## Claude Cowork: Your AI Teammate for the Modern Workplace
23 June 2026 · 7 min read · By Okiru Consulting
Artificial Intelligence has evolved rapidly over the last few years.
The first wave of AI focused on answering questions. The second wave focused on generating content. Now, we're entering a new phase where AI is becoming an active participant in the workplace.
One of the most interesting examples of this shift is Claude Cowork , Anthropic's new agentic AI workspace designed to help professionals move beyond conversations and toward real task execution. Instead of simply responding to prompts, Claude Cowork can assist with ongoing projects, interact with files, analyse data, automate workflows, and support teams across multiple business functions.
## What Is Claude Cowork?
Claude Cowork is Anthropic's enterprise-focused AI work environment that allows users to collaborate with Claude on real business tasks. Unlike a traditional chatbot experience, Cowork is designed around ongoing work, projects, and workflows rather than isolated conversations.
Anthropic describes Cowork as a way to work alongside Claude on real files and projects, helping individuals and teams integrate AI into their daily operations.
Recent updates have expanded Claude Cowork's capabilities to include:
Access to enterprise data and files
Workflow automation
Long-running task execution
Computer interaction capabilities
Team collaboration features
Enterprise governance and analytics controls
The goal is simple: transform AI from a tool you consult into a teammate you collaborate with.
## How Claude Cowork Differs From Traditional AI Chatbots
Most AI tools operate in a request-response model. You ask a question. The AI provides an answer.
Claude Cowork introduces a more collaborative approach. Rather than only generating responses, it can:
Work across multiple files
Maintain project context
Execute multi-step tasks
Assist with ongoing workflows
Integrate with enterprise systems
Operate across departments and teams
This makes it particularly valuable for organisations looking to embed AI into everyday operations rather than treating it as a standalone productivity tool.
## Best Use Cases for Claude Cowork
## 1. Research and Strategic Analysis
One of Claude's strongest capabilities is processing and analysing large volumes of information. Teams can use Cowork to:
Analyse industry reports
Summarise research documents
Identify trends and patterns
Prepare executive briefings
Conduct competitor analysis
For leadership teams, this can significantly reduce the time required to gather and synthesise information.
## 2. Financial and Business Reporting
Anthropic highlights financial analysis as a key use case, with Cowork able to work with spreadsheets, financial statements, and business documents to assist with reporting and analysis. Examples include:
Performance reporting
Budget reviews
Investment analysis
Forecasting support
Executive dashboards
## 3. Project Management Support
Claude Cowork can help teams manage projects by organising information, tracking action items, summarising meetings, and maintaining visibility across initiatives.
Instead of manually compiling updates, project teams can use AI to consolidate information and generate status reports automatically.
## 4. Content Creation and Marketing
Marketing teams can leverage Claude Cowork to:
Draft blog articles
Create social media campaigns
Develop email content
Generate reports
Repurpose existing content
Because Claude can maintain context across larger projects, it can support more consistent content development workflows.
## 5. HR and Employee Enablement
Human Resources teams can use Claude Cowork for:
Policy development
Employee onboarding materials
Training content
Internal communications
Knowledge management
This allows HR teams to focus more on people and less on repetitive administrative work.
## 6. Sales and Customer Success
Sales teams are increasingly using AI to accelerate research and customer engagement. Claude Cowork can assist with:
Account research
Proposal development
Customer meeting preparation
Opportunity analysis
Follow-up communications
This helps teams spend more time building relationships and less time preparing documentation.
## 7. Knowledge Management
One of the most powerful enterprise use cases is organisational knowledge management.
Claude can connect with business information sources and help employees quickly find answers, summarise documents, and retrieve relevant information. Anthropic has also introduced integrations with workplace platforms such as Microsoft 365 services to improve access to organisational knowledge.
## Why Claude Cowork Matters
Research from Anthropic suggests that AI usage is increasingly shifting toward higher levels of autonomy, with users delegating more tasks to AI systems over time. Recent analysis of millions of Claude interactions found growing use of AI for both augmentation and automation across professional work.
Claude Cowork reflects this broader trend. Rather than simply helping users create content faster, it aims to become part of how work gets done. For organisations, this represents a significant opportunity to:
Increase productivity
Reduce repetitive work
Improve decision-making
Accelerate project delivery
Enhance knowledge sharing
Scale expertise across teams
## Key Considerations Before Adoption
While Claude Cowork offers substantial benefits, successful implementation requires more than deploying technology. Organisations should consider:
AI governance policies
Data privacy requirements
Employee training
Workflow redesign
Change management
Responsible AI practices
The most successful AI deployments focus on enabling people rather than replacing them. AI works best when combined with human expertise, judgment, and oversight.
## The Future of Work Is Collaborative
The future workplace is unlikely to be fully human or fully automated. Instead, it will be built around collaboration between people and intelligent systems.
Claude Cowork represents an important step in that direction. By transforming AI from a chatbot into a collaborative teammate, organisations have the opportunity to rethink how work is organised, how knowledge is shared, and how teams operate.
The question is no longer whether AI will become part of the workplace. The question is how effectively organisations will learn to work alongside it.
## How Okiru Can Help Your Team Adopt AI Successfully
Technology alone does not create transformation. People do.
Okiru provides practical AI Training and Enablement programmes that help organisations build the skills, governance, and confidence needed to use AI effectively. Our training covers:
AI Fundamentals for Business
Prompt Engineering
AI Productivity Workflows
Responsible AI & Governance
Department-Specific AI Use Cases
AI Strategy & Adoption Roadmaps
Whether you're exploring tools like Claude, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, or other AI platforms, Okiru can help your team move from experimentation to measurable business value.
Contact Okiru today to learn how AI training can help your organisation unlock the full potential of AI-powered work.

# https://okiru.biz/blog/claude-fable-5-what-anthropic-s-most-powerful-ai-yet-means-for-your-business/

**Page title:** Claude Fable 5: What Anthropic's Most Powerful AI Yet Means for Your Business | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is the most capable AI model ever released to the public — and it just became available worldwide. Here's what the new Mythos-class era means for South African businesses.

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
Claude Fable 5: What Anthropic's Most Powerful AI Yet Means for Your Business
AI Trends
## Claude Fable 5: What Anthropic's Most Powerful AI Yet Means for Your Business
2 July 2026 · 11 min read · By Okiru Consulting
Every so often, an AI release changes the conversation. Not because of the benchmark charts — those blur together after a while — but because it forces businesses to ask a different question. Anthropic's release of Claude Fable 5 is one of those moments.
Launched on 9 June 2026 and made available globally from 1 July, Fable 5 is the first of what Anthropic calls a Mythos-class model — a new tier of AI capability that sits above anything the company has previously released to the public. In Anthropic's own words, Fable 5's capabilities "exceed those of any model we've ever made generally available."
That is not marketing hyperbole. The three weeks between launch and global availability included something unprecedented: the US government temporarily placed export controls on the model before lifting them at the end of June. Whatever your view on AI regulation, when a commercial software product is briefly treated like controlled technology, it tells you the ground has shifted.
So what does this actually mean for your business — especially here in South Africa? Let's unpack it.
## What Exactly Is Claude Fable 5?
Fable 5 is Anthropic's fifth-generation frontier model and the new flagship of the Claude family. A few things set it apart from every Claude model before it:
A new capability tier. Anthropic now describes its top models as "Mythos-class". Fable 5 is the version of that tier made safe for general use — available on the Claude apps, Claude Code, the API, and major cloud platforms.
It works for days, not minutes. In agent environments, Fable 5 can plan multi-stage projects, delegate to sub-agents, and check its own work over extended periods with limited supervision.
A 1-million-token context window with always-on adaptive thinking. In practical terms: it can hold an entire company's policy library, a full financial year of reports, or a massive codebase in its head at once — and it decides for itself how hard to think about each step.
State-of-the-art vision. It can extract precise figures from dense charts and even rebuild working software from screenshots alone.
During early testing, payments company Stripe reported that Fable 5 compressed months of engineering into days — completing a migration across a 50-million-line codebase in a single day, work that would otherwise have taken a full team more than two months.
Pause on that for a moment. Not a demo. Not a lab benchmark. A production system at one of the world's largest fintech companies.
## The Export Control Episode — and Why It Matters
Three days after launch, the US government applied export controls to Fable 5 and its unrestricted sibling, Mythos 5, following concerns about jailbreak techniques that could bypass the model's cybersecurity safeguards. Anthropic suspended access entirely rather than risk non-compliance. By 30 June the controls were lifted, safeguards were strengthened, and the model was restored worldwide.
It would be easy to file this under "American politics" and move on. That would be a mistake, for two reasons.
First, it confirms that frontier AI is now a matter of national strategy. Governments are no longer debating whether advanced AI matters — they are actively managing who gets access to it and under what conditions. South African businesses that depend on international AI providers should understand that access to the very best models can be interrupted by geopolitics, even briefly. That is a new category of operational risk, and it belongs in your risk register alongside load shedding and currency volatility.
Second, it shows the safeguards are real. Fable 5 ships with safety classifiers that route certain sensitive queries to a less capable model. Anthropic, Amazon, Microsoft and Google are now building a shared industry framework for assessing jailbreaks. For business leaders who have been hesitant about AI because of safety concerns, the governance infrastructure around these models is maturing quickly — imperfectly, but visibly.
## From Assistant to Colleague: The Real Shift
The most important thing about Fable 5 is not any single capability. It is the change in the unit of work you can hand to an AI.
With previous generations, the unit of work was a task: draft this email, summarise this document, write this function. With Fable 5, the unit of work is a project : analyse our customer churn and produce a board-ready report; migrate this system; review every supplier contract against our new procurement policy and flag the exceptions.
This is the difference between an assistant and a colleague. And it changes how you should think about deploying AI in your organisation:
Delegation becomes a skill. The businesses that benefit most will be those whose people know how to brief an AI the way they would brief a capable new hire — with context, constraints, and clear definitions of done.
Review becomes the bottleneck. When AI can produce days of work overnight, the scarce resource is no longer production — it is the judgement to evaluate what has been produced. Senior expertise becomes more valuable, not less.
Process documentation becomes an asset. A model with a million-token context window can absorb your entire operations manual. Companies with well-documented processes will get dramatically more out of these tools than companies that keep their knowledge in people's heads.
The question is no longer "what can AI do?" It is "what work in my business is well-defined enough to delegate?"
## What Can You Actually Do With Fable 5?
Setting aside the headlines, here is where Mythos-class capability translates into practical business value:
1. Deep document work. Fable 5 posted the highest score of any model on Hebbia's Finance Benchmark for senior-level analytical reasoning — reading dense documents, interpreting charts and tables, and reasoning across them. For any business drowning in contracts, tender documents, compliance submissions or financial reports, this is the headline capability. Think B-BBEE verification files, WSP/ATR submissions, or due diligence packs.
2. Software and systems modernisation. The Stripe example is the template: legacy system migrations, codebase-wide upgrades, and technical debt cleanup that companies have deferred for years because the engineering cost was prohibitive. If your business runs on ageing software, the economics of fixing it just changed.
3. Research and analysis at scale. Trading firm IMC reported that Fable 5 essentially aced its internal evaluations across factual lookup, conceptual reasoning, root-cause analysis and expected-value analysis. Market research, competitor analysis, regulatory horizon-scanning — work that used to take an analyst a week can now be a same-day turnaround with human review.
4. Vision-driven workflows. Because Fable 5 can read screenshots, scanned documents, and complex figures with precision, workflows that were previously "too messy" for AI — invoice processing from photographed documents, extracting data from legacy PDF reports, digitising paper-based processes — become viable.
5. Long-running autonomous projects. With appropriate oversight, Fable 5 can be given a goal and left to work: monitoring, reconciling, drafting, and escalating only when it needs a decision. This is where the "AI as digital teammate" idea stops being a metaphor.
## The Pricing Reality — and the Sonnet 5 Alternative
Frontier capability comes at frontier prices. Fable 5 is priced at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens on the API — less than half the price of its preview predecessor, but still a premium product. On Claude's paid plans, it is available within usage limits and through usage credits.
Here is the strategic nuance most commentary misses: on 30 June — the very same week — Anthropic also released Claude Sonnet 5 , a far cheaper model ($2/$10 per million tokens on promotion) that outperforms last year's flagship on most business tasks and carries the same 1-million-token context window.
For most South African businesses, the right architecture is a portfolio, not a single model :
Use an efficient model like Sonnet 5 for the everyday 90% — drafting, summarising, customer responses, routine analysis.
Reserve Fable 5 for the high-stakes 10% — complex analysis, major migrations, work where the quality delta justifies the cost.
This mirrors how you staff a business. You don't put your most senior specialist on every task; you deploy them where their judgement moves the needle.
## The South African Angle: Leapfrog or Lag
There is a tempting narrative that frontier AI is a rich-world story — relevant to Silicon Valley, remote from the realities of running a business in South Africa. The opposite is true, for three reasons.
Skills leverage. South Africa's most persistent business constraint is scarce specialist skills — engineers, analysts, compliance experts. Models like Fable 5 don't replace those people; they multiply them. A single experienced professional supervising AI-driven work can now cover ground that previously required a team. For a mid-sized South African firm competing against better-resourced international rivals, that is leverage, not threat.
The cost curve favours followers. Fable 5 launched at less than half the price of the model it replaced, and Sonnet 5 delivers most of the capability at a fifth of the cost. Businesses that build AI-ready processes now — clean data, documented workflows, clear governance — will ride that cost curve down. The capability you can't afford this quarter will be affordable next year, but only if your organisation is ready to use it.
Governance is coming here too. Between POPIA obligations, sector regulators watching AI closely, and the global trend the export-control episode revealed, South African boards should treat AI governance as a present-tense responsibility. The companies that establish clear policies on AI use, data handling, and human oversight now will adopt each new model generation in weeks. The ones that don't will spend every release cycle relitigating the basics.
## What Should You Do This Quarter?
You don't need a moonshot. You need a deliberate start:
Pick one project-sized problem — not a task, a project. A backlog analysis, a document review, a system migration scoping exercise. Run it with a frontier model and measure the result honestly.
Audit your documentation. The businesses that win with million-token context windows are the ones with something worth putting in them. If your processes live in people's heads, start writing them down.
Decide your model portfolio. Match everyday work to efficient models and reserve frontier capability for high-value use cases. Review quarterly — the price-performance frontier is moving fast.
Put AI on the risk register. Provider concentration, access interruptions, data governance, output review. Treat it like any other critical dependency.
Invest in your reviewers. The scarce skill of the next five years is not prompting — it is the domain judgement to evaluate AI-produced work. Develop it deliberately.
## The Bottom Line
Claude Fable 5 is a milestone, but the milestone is not the model. It is what the model represents: AI has crossed from assisting with tasks to executing projects, and the world's governments have noticed.
For South African businesses, the window is genuinely open. The capability is available globally, the cost curve is falling, and the playbook — start small, document well, govern clearly, review rigorously — is the same whether you are in Sandton, Stellenbosch or Silicon Valley.
The businesses that treat this as a quarter-by-quarter capability-building exercise will look back on 2026 as the year they got ahead. The ones waiting for the technology to "settle down" should know: it isn't going to.
Claude Fable 5 Anthropic Claude AI Mythos-class AI Agentic AI Frontier AI AI for Business South Africa AI Adoption AI Strategy Claude Sonnet 5 Enterprise AI AI Governance Future of Work

**Page title:** Claude Is Retiring Three Features: Here's What's Changing Before June 15 | Okiru Blog
**Meta description:** Anthropic has announced that three Claude features will be gradually retired by June 15 as the platform moves to a more capable, VM-based architecture. Most migrations happen automatically — but some workflows now depend on code execution and file creation being enabled. Here's what's changing and what to check before

AI Tool Advisor
All Tools
Advisor
Blog
Home ›
Blog ›
Claude Is Retiring Three Features: Here's What's Changing Before June 15
Product Updates
## Claude Is Retiring Three Features: Here's What's Changing Before June 15
13 May 2026 · 4 min read · By Okiru Consulting
Anthropic has announced that three Claude features will be gradually retired by 15 June as the platform moves toward a more capable and reliable VM-based architecture.
The good news is that most migrations will happen automatically. The main change users may notice is that some workflows now depend on code execution and file creation being enabled. Here's a breakdown of what's changing and what it means for Claude users.
## 1. Artifacts are moving to a VM-based experience
Existing Artifacts will automatically migrate to VM-based Artifacts, so there's nothing users need to manually move or recreate. However, creating and interacting with Artifacts going forward will require code execution and file creation to be enabled .
The upgraded environment supports creating and editing downloadable files such as spreadsheets, presentations, documents, and PDFs directly inside Claude — meaningfully expanding what you can produce in a single conversation.
## 2. Styles are being replaced with Skills
Custom Styles are also being migrated automatically — this time into Skills . Skills are becoming the new way to customise Claude's behaviour and workflows. Users who previously relied on Styles will continue using similar functionality through Skills, provided code execution and file creation are enabled.
Anthropic also confirmed that the Learning Mode style will migrate into a Skill. For teams and organisations, some workflows can alternatively be handled through custom instructions set at an organisational level.
## 3. Project file search is changing
Claude is also improving how Projects search and use uploaded files. Under the new architecture, Project file search will depend on the same VM-based execution environment, meaning code execution and file creation must be enabled for the feature to work fully.
According to Anthropic, project files remain available in Claude's context while also becoming accessible through the computing environment — allowing for more advanced workflows across uploaded documents and datasets.
## Why Anthropic is making the change
Anthropic says the move is part of a broader shift toward a more capable and reliable architecture built around sandboxed virtual machine execution . The company has continued investing in governance and security controls around this environment, including configurable network access and restricted domain access.
Anthropic's engineering team has also shared details on how Claude's sandboxing works, including filesystem isolation and network isolation designed to reduce risk during code execution. In short: the platform is being re-platformed onto something that can do much more, more safely.
## What users should do before 15 June
Most users won't need to manually migrate anything, but it's worth checking whether code execution and file creation are enabled if you regularly use:
Artifacts
Custom Styles
Project file search
Advanced document workflows
Without those capabilities enabled, some migrated features may no longer function as expected after the retirement deadline. For organisations on Team or Enterprise plans, this is also a good moment to review which capabilities are turned on at the workspace level — settings can vary by admin policy.
## Final thoughts
This update signals a bigger shift in how Claude is evolving. Rather than treating features like Artifacts and file search as standalone tools, Anthropic is consolidating them into a unified VM-powered workflow experience . For users, that likely means more capable automation, richer document handling, and more advanced AI-assisted workflows going forward.
If you'd like help auditing your team's Claude setup before the June 15 deadline — or designing workflows that take advantage of the new VM-based capabilities — the Okiru team is here to help. Get in touch via okiru.co.za .
